title: Stillness is safe. I am safe
subtitle: An affirmation for when rest feels like it carries a risk.
excerpt: A short affirmation for bedtime, for when stopping and lying still in the dark reads as dangerous rather than restful. Say it as a deliberate instruction each time the urge to get up arrives.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 5 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Feel safe in stillness."

content.text: A short affirmation for bedtime. Use it each time the body sends up the signal to move, check, or get up. The statement is a direct instruction: stillness is safe. You are safe.
Stillness is safe. I am safe. I can lie here and nothing bad will happen.
Say it in your mind, slowly, each time the urge to move arrives. Don't try to believe it; just repeat it. The repetition is how the nervous system learns that the statement is true.
